$comment: Golden cases for place-name folding, which the core and the mock gateway each implement (Rust: voyalier-core::packs::normalize_place; TypeScript: mock.ts mockNormalizePlace). Destinations are user-typed free text, so the two disagreeing means a pack matches in one and not the other. voyalier-core's parity_normalize_place_matches_the_contract and apps/web's parity.test.ts both assert against this list. Add a case here before fixing a folding bug.

cases.$why: ʻokina and apostrophes join the word rather than splitting it
lowercase accents fold to their base letter
uppercase accents fold too — the core matched only A-Z, so these became word separators
o-slash and sharp-s do not decompose under NFKD, so the mock dropped them
unfoldable scripts separate words rather than vanishing
